evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix with dual-colormap (YlOrRd diagonal, Blues off-diagonal) and full cell annotation clearly conveys classifier performance as specified in the brief.
evidence.typography: pdffonts reports Type 3 DejaVuSans and DejaVuSans-Bold; script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0, so text is bitmapped and non-editable.
evidence.palette: Blues and YlOrRd are both colorblind-safe sequential colormaps; no red/green directional misuse, though two separate sequential scales for one matrix may confuse readers.
evidence.layout_density: 8x7 in figure with tight_layout for a 10x10 grid is adequately dense; colorbar present; no label overlap observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes the figure and color encoding, but there is no colorbar or legend for the diagonal YlOrRd scale — Reviewer 2 would flag this asymmetry.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains only Type 3 fonts (confirmed by pdffonts); text is not selectable or editable; script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (30 KB), figure.png (65 KB), figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial; data.json is an input, not a required output.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies row/column semantics, explains dual-colormap encoding, notes bold white diagonal text, and provides a specific numerical finding (per-class accuracy 69% C1 to 92% C8).

overall_strengths: Dual-colormap design cleanly separates correct predictions (diagonal) from errors (off-diagonal), making the matrix immediately interpretable.
Caption is publication-quality: it explains the visual encoding and anchors the figure with a concrete numerical claim.
overall_weaknesses: No pdf.fonttype=42 set — all text is rendered as Type 3 bitmaps, making the PDF unsuitable for journal submission or editing.
Diagonal YlOrRd colormap has no corresponding colorbar, leaving readers unable to interpret the diagonal color scale quantitatively.
